title: Bridge Essay: Effects of compassion training on brain responses to suffering others.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Formless/Absorption** (Phase 8) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Formless/Absorption to mechanistic neuroscience.

**Effects of compassion training on brain responses to suffering others.** — Soc Cogn Affect Neurosci. PMID: 33948660. DOI: 10.1093/scan/nsab052. Compassion meditation (CM) is apromising intervention for enhancing compassion, although its active ingredients and neurobiological mechanisms are not well-understood. To investigate these, we conducted athree-armed placebo-controlled randomized trial (N = 57) with longitudinal functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI). We compared a 4-week CM program delivered by smartphone application with (i) aplacebo condition, presented to participants as the compassion-enhancing hormone oxytocin, and
